info.title: UseZombie Control Plane API
info.description: Zombie automation control plane — webhook-driven agent execution in a bwrap+landlock sandbox.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: CLI login flow via device-code style sessions and GitHub OAuth

tags.name: Workspaces
tags.description: Create and manage workspaces (one workspace per repo)

tags.name: Tenant
tags.description: Tenant-scoped surfaces — provider posture, doctor, and tenant-level configuration

tags.name: Runs
tags.description: Core spec-to-PR pipeline — queue, monitor, stream, retry, and cancel runs

tags.name: Zombies
tags.description: Zombie management and webhook ingestion — create, configure, and operate event-driven Zombies

tags.name: Memory
tags.description: Persistent zombie memory — store, recall, list, and forget key-value entries scoped to a zombie

tags.name: Credentials
tags.description: BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) LLM provider credentials

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Platform-wide configuration — manage shared LLM provider keys

tags.name: Integration Grants
tags.description: Zombie runtime permission grants for third-party services (Slack, GitHub, etc.)

tags.name: Agent Keys
tags.description: Per-zombie API keys for LangGraph/CrewAI/Composio agents to call the Execute endpoint. Workspace-scoped.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Tenant-level admin API keys (`zmb_t_` prefix). Rotatable, self-service, replace the legacy `API_KEY` env var for admin auth.

tags.name: Execute
tags.description: Synchronous tool execution proxy for external agents

tags.name: Slack
tags.description: Slack OAuth install flow and inbound events/interactions webhooks

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Approval-gate webhooks invoked by humans to unblock a paused zombie

tags.name: Telemetry
tags.description: Per-zombie execution telemetry and internal operator telemetry

tags.name: Approvals
tags.description: Approval-gate inbox — list, inspect, and resolve pending gates from the dashboard or programmatically.

get.description: Returns 200 as long as the process is alive and able to serve HTTP. Does not probe dependencies — use /readyz for readiness (database + queue).

commit.description: Git commit SHA of the running build

get.description: Admin-only platform health snapshot — in-flight request counts, queue depth, error rates. Requires the admin API key, not a user bearer token.
Returns Prometheus-format metrics for scraping by monitoring infrastructure.
Kubernetes-style readiness probe. Returns 200 when both the database and the Redis queue are reachable; returns 503 fail-closed if either dependency is degraded.

503.description: At least one dependency is degraded; ready=false and the failing dependency's boolean is false

get.description: Lists all configured platform-wide LLM provider keys. These are shared keys that workspaces fall back to when no BYOK key is configured.

put.description: Configures a platform-wide LLM provider key by referencing a workspace that has a BYOK key stored. The platform key proxies through the referenced workspace's credential.

source_workspace_id.description: Workspace whose BYOK key to use as the platform key

delete.description: Removes a platform-wide LLM provider key. Workspaces without BYOK keys will no longer be able to use this provider.

post.description: Creates a device-code style auth session. Returns a session ID and a login URL to present to the user in their browser.

login_url.description: URL to open in the user's browser to complete authentication

get.description: Polls a pending auth session. The CLI calls this repeatedly until status transitions to 'complete' (token available) or 'expired'.

token.description: Bearer token, present only when status is 'complete'

patch.description: Called by the web frontend after successful OAuth to attach a token to the pending session and transition its status to 'complete'. The CLI's next poll will receive the token. Body mirrors the GET poll response shape so the depositor can read back what a subsequent poll would return.

status.description: Session transition target. Only 'complete' is legal from 'pending'.

token.description: Bearer token now attached to the session

post.summary: Execute a tool on behalf of an external agent
post.description: Synchronous tool execution proxy. The external agent calls this endpoint with its API key (bearer), names a tool, and receives the tool's result inline. Used by LangGraph / CrewAI / Composio integrations.

target.description: Target URL for the outbound call.

method.description: HTTP method to use against the target.

credential_ref.description: Named credential from the workspace vault to inject on the outbound request.

body.description: Request body to forward to the target. Null for GET/DELETE.

200.description: Tool executed; upstream status and body are returned plus UseZombie metadata.

status.description: HTTP status returned by the upstream target.

body.description: Response body returned by the upstream target (as string).

get.summary: List or search memory entries for a zombie
get.description: Returns memory entries for the zombie. Without `query` this lists most-recent entries (optionally filtered by `category`). With `query`, performs case-insensitive LIKE search across both `key` and `content` (LIKE metacharacters in `query` are escaped — a query of `%` does NOT return every entry).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Fuzzy text search across key and content. When omitted, the endpoint lists most-recent entries.

post.summary: Store or upsert a memory entry
post.description: Writes a `(key, content, category)` entry scoped to the zombie. Idempotent upsert on `(key, instance_id)`.

delete.summary: Forget a memory entry by key
delete.description: Removes the entry at `(zombie_id, memory_key)` if present. Strictly idempotent — returns 204 whether the key existed or not.

204.description: No Content (idempotent — returned whether the key existed or not)

post.description: Ingress for Clerk `user.created` events delivered via Svix. The signature is verified inline against `CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET`; on success the handler atomically provisions a personal account (tenant + user + owner membership + default workspace + 0-cent credit state + audit row). Idempotent on `oidc_subject` — replays return `created:false` with no new rows. Non-`user.created` event types are 200-ignored so Clerk stops retrying them.

parameters.name: svix-id
parameters.description: Svix message identifier (used for deduplication and signature binding).

parameters.name: svix-timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of the Svix delivery (used for replay protection).

parameters.name: svix-signature
parameters.description: Space-separated list of Svix v1 signatures.

type.description: Clerk event type. Only `user.created` triggers a bootstrap; other types return 200 ignored.

data.description: Clerk user payload. The handler reads `id`, `email_addresses`, `primary_email_address_id`, `first_name`, and `last_name`; unknown fields are tolerated.

200.description: Account bootstrapped (fresh or replayed). `created` is `true` on first delivery and `false` on every subsequent replay of the same `oidc_subject`.

workspace_id.description: UUIDv7 of the newly provisioned default workspace (or the existing one on replay).

created.description: `true` on fresh bootstrap; `false` on idempotent replay.

post.description: Ingress endpoint for Svix-signed webhooks (e.g. Clerk user lifecycle events). Signature is verified by the webhook auth middleware against the workspace's Svix secret before the handler runs. Body shape is event-specific and defined by the upstream issuer; the server validates the envelope and dispatches to the zombie's event log.

parameters.name: zombie_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the zombie receiving the webhook.

parameters.name: svix-id
parameters.description: Svix message identifier (used for deduplication and signature binding).

parameters.name: svix-timestamp
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of the Svix delivery (used for replay protection).

parameters.name: svix-signature
parameters.description: Space-separated list of Svix signatures (v1 scheme).

schema.description: Opaque event envelope; upstream issuer (Svix) defines the payload shape per event type.

202.description: Webhook accepted and queued for processing.

post.summary: Deliver an inbound webhook event to a Zombie
post.description: Delivers an inbound event to the Zombie identified by `zombie_id`. The Zombie is looked up by primary key — no JSONB traversal, no source-name uniqueness required. The body MUST be HMAC-SHA256 signed with the workspace credential keyed by the Zombie's `trigger.source` (e.g. vault row `zombie:linear`, field `webhook_secret`). The provider-specific signature header (`x-hub-signature-256` for GitHub, `x-slack-signature` for Slack, etc.) is verified upstream by the `webhook_sig` middleware; this handler runs only after the HMAC is valid. There is no Bearer fallback — every inbound webhook MUST be signed. Duplicate events (same `event_id` within 24 h) return 200 `{status: "duplicate"}`. A new event is enqueued to the Zombie's Redis stream and returns 202.

parameters.name: zombie_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the target Zombie. Obtained from `POST /v1/workspaces/{workspace_id}/zombies` or `zombiectl install --from <path>`.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event. Used for idempotency deduplication (24-hour window).

data.description: Event payload — any JSON object. Passed as-is into the Zombie event loop.

200.description: Duplicate event — already processed within the 24-hour dedup window.

202.description: Event accepted and enqueued for the Zombie.

401.description: Webhook auth failure. Three distinct codes: `UZ-WH-020 webhook_credential_not_configured` (zombie has no recognized provider, OR the workspace has no matching `zombie:<source>` vault credential, OR the credential JSON has no `webhook_secret` field — this is the operator-recoverable misconfig path); `UZ-WH-010 invalid_signature` (provider + secret are configured but the request is unsigned, mis-signed, or the body was tampered with); `UZ-WH-011 stale_timestamp` (Slack-style schemes with timestamp drift > 5 min).

404.description: No active Zombie is configured for this source (UZ-WH-001).

409.description: Zombie exists but is paused or stopped (UZ-WH-003).

post.description: Called by a human (via email link or Slack action) to approve or reject a paused zombie. Body is HMAC-signed by the issuer; the signature is verified against the zombie's webhook secret.

post.summary: Approve or reject an integration grant request
post.description: Human-in-the-loop approval webhook for integration grant requests (Slack, GitHub, etc.). HMAC-signed.

GitHub `workflow_run` webhook receiver. Body is HMAC-SHA256 signed (X-Hub-Signature-256) against the workspace's `zombie:github` credential `webhook_secret` field. Only `action=completed` with `conclusion=failure` is XADDed to the zombie event stream; other events return 204. Idempotency keyed on `X-GitHub-Delivery` for 24h.

parameters.name: zombie_id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the zombie that owns this webhook receiver.

parameters.name: X-GitHub-Event
parameters.description: GitHub event type. Only `workflow_run` is ingested.

parameters.name: X-GitHub-Delivery
parameters.description: GitHub-issued delivery UUID. Used as the dedupe key.

parameters.name: X-Hub-Signature-256
parameters.description: HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body, prefixed with `sha256=`.

200.description: Duplicate delivery (idempotency hit) or event ignored (non-workflow_run, non-completed action, or non-failure conclusion). Body distinguishes the two via the `deduped` or `ignored` field.

ignored.description: Reason the event was filtered out (e.g. `non_failure_conclusion`).

post.description: Creates a new workspace linked to a GitHub repository. Returns the workspace ID and a GitHub App install URL if the app is not yet installed on the repo.

default_branch.description: Default branch name, defaults to 'main' if omitted

install_url.description: GitHub App installation URL — direct user here if the app is not yet installed

get.description: Returns the tenant-scoped billing row created at signup. The single row carries the plan tier, SKU, and free-credit balance shared across every workspace the tenant owns. Debited by the worker as zombie runs complete.

is_exhausted.description: True once the balance has hit zero on a worker debit.

exhausted_at.description: Epoch ms of the first exhausting debit. Null until the balance is exhausted.

get.description: Returns the newest-first slice of `zombie_execution_telemetry` rows for the caller's tenant. Two rows per event under the credit-pool model (`charge_type` ∈ `receive`, `stage`); the dashboard groups them by `event_id` to show one line per event with both charges. Limit-only paging is sufficient for the v2.0 Settings → Billing Usage tab; deeper history pages with Stripe billing in v2.1.

200.description: Newest-first telemetry rows scoped to the caller's tenant.

get.summary: List every workspace the caller's tenant owns
get.description: Returns the full set of workspaces owned by the caller's tenant. Backs the dashboard workspace switcher: a user can switch to any workspace in this list without re-issuing the JWT, because workspace authorisation is tenant-scoped. No pagination — a tenant's workspace count is bounded and small in practice.

name.description: Human-readable workspace name. Nullable — pre-rename workspaces may not have one.

get.description: Returns the resolver's view of the tenant's LLM provider posture as a `tenant_provider` block. Covers both platform-managed (synthesised default OR explicit reset) and BYOK modes. The api_key is never serialised. User-fixable BYOK errors (credential row missing, malformed credential JSON) surface as an `error_label` field rather than a 5xx so the client doctor can report them as actionable findings; operator-side errors (platform key missing, tenant has no workspace) still 5xx.

provider.description: Provider name (e.g. fireworks, anthropic, openai). Under platform mode, read from the active core.platform_llm_keys row.

context_cap_tokens.description: Resolved context window cap in tokens.

credential_ref.description: User-named vault key holding the BYOK credential. Null under platform mode.

error_label.description: Set when BYOK resolution failed at a user-fixable point — credential row missing from vault, or stored JSON missing required fields. Null on success.

get.description: Returns the tenant's persisted provider config — mode, provider name, model, context cap, and (under BYOK) the user-named credential_ref. The api_key is never serialised. Tenants with no row receive a synthesised platform default.

put.description: Eager-validates and UPSERTs the tenant_providers row. Validation order: body shape → credential_ref required (mode=byok) → credential_not_found → credential_data_malformed → model_not_in_caps_catalogue → UPSERT. Stored `provider` is read from the credential JSON; effective `model` is the body override (when present) else the credential's stored model.

credential_ref.description: User-named vault key. Required when mode=byok.

model.description: Optional model override. Falls back to the credential's stored model when absent.

delete.summary: Reset tenant LLM provider to platform default
delete.description: Equivalent to `PUT {mode: "platform"}`. Writes the explicit mode=platform row so the dashboard can distinguish "never configured" from "explicitly reset".

get.summary: List credentials stored for a workspace
get.description: Returns credential names. Values are never returned — they live encrypted in `vault.secrets`.

post.summary: Store a credential in the workspace vault
post.description:
Plaintext is the canonical-stringified form of the supplied JSON object,
envelope-encrypted with the KEK (master key v1) and persisted to
`vault.secrets`. The raw value is never stored unencrypted and never
returned. `data` must be a non-empty JSON object whose stringified form
is ≤ 4 KiB; bare strings, arrays, scalars, and `{}` are rejected with
`UZ-VAULT-001`.

name.description: Credential identifier referenced from a zombie's `credentials:` list.

data.description:
JSON object whose top-level keys are the field names a skill
references via `${secrets.<name>.<field>}`. The runtime never
inspects the schema — this is opaque to the platform.

delete.summary: Delete a credential from the workspace vault
delete.description: Idempotent — returns 204 whether or not the credential existed.

post.description: Creates a new `zmb_t_`-prefixed tenant admin key. **The raw key is returned once.** Only the SHA-256 hash is persisted — the key cannot be retrieved again. Requires operator (or admin) role.

key_name.description: Unique per tenant. Alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores only.

key.description: Raw `zmb_t_` tenant key (70 chars). Shown once — store securely.

409.description: Key name already exists for this tenant (`UZ-APIKEY-005`).

get.description: Returns metadata for every tenant API key visible to the caller. Results are always scoped to the caller's tenant; `key_hash` is never returned.

total.description: Total rows across all pages for this tenant.

patch.description: Partial lifecycle update. Body must be `{"active": false}` — re-activation is not supported; mint a new key instead. Sets `revoked_at` and `updated_at`.

409.description: Key is already revoked (`UZ-APIKEY-006`) or attempted to set `active: true` (`UZ-APIKEY-007`).

delete.summary: Delete a revoked tenant API key
delete.description: Hard-deletes the row from `core.api_keys`. The key must already be revoked (`active = false`); an active key returns `409 UZ-APIKEY-008`.

409.description: Active key must be revoked before deletion (`UZ-APIKEY-008`).

get.summary: List agent keys in a workspace
get.description: Returns agent-key metadata only — never returns `key_hash`.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

post.summary: Issue an agent key for a zombie
post.description: Creates a new `zmb_` agent key bound to a specific zombie. **The raw key is returned once.** Only the SHA-256 hash is persisted — the key cannot be retrieved again.

key.description: Raw `zmb_` API key. Shown once — store securely.

delete.description: Revokes the agent key immediately. Subsequent requests with the key receive 401.

get.description: Returns zombies the caller has access to in the given workspace. Keyset paginated over `(created_at DESC, id DESC)`. Omit `cursor` for the first page; pass the response `cursor` back verbatim to fetch the next page. `cursor` is `null` when the last page has been reached. Response envelope follows the RAD §8 `items`/`total` convention (`total` is the size of this page, not a global count).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque page cursor from a prior response's `cursor` field, formatted `{created_at_epoch_ms}:{zombie_id}`. Omit for the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Defaults to 20. Clamped to a maximum of 100.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

cursor.description: Opaque cursor to fetch the next page. `null` when there are no more pages.

post.description: Creates a new zombie from a TRIGGER.md (deployment manifest) and a SKILL.md (agent instructions). The server is the single parser of TRIGGER.md frontmatter — `name` and the persisted `config_json` are derived server-side. The CLI (`zombiectl install --from <path>`) sends the two markdown files as raw bytes; no YAML parsing happens client-side.

trigger_markdown.description: TRIGGER.md raw bytes — YAML frontmatter (`name`, `trigger`, `tools`, `credentials`, `network`, `budget`, `gates`) between `---` delimiters. Server parses this to derive `name` and the persisted `config_json`.

source_markdown.description: SKILL.md raw bytes — the agent instructions, stored as-is.

name.description: Zombie name parsed from TRIGGER.md frontmatter — the canonical identifier the user will see in `zombiectl status` and CLI output.

patch.description: Partial update of a zombie. Body fields are optional and presence-based: `config_json` (replace the zombie's config — publishes zombie_config_changed) and/or `status:"killed"` (cancel any in-flight executor session and mark the zombie killed — publishes zombie_status_changed). Both fields can ride together; the SQL UPDATE is one statement and one control-stream publish fires per dirty surface. Returns the new config_revision (a strictly monotonic ms-epoch timestamp). Idempotent — re-killing a killed zombie returns 404.

config_json.description: The new zombie config as a JSON string. Validated server-side.

status.description: Set to "killed" to cancel any in-flight session and mark the zombie killed.
Present only when the request body included `status:"killed"`.

get.summary: List integration grants for a zombie
get.description: Returns pending, approved, and revoked grants, newest first.

total.description: Count of items in this response (not total across pages).

delete.description: Immediately revokes a grant. A revoked grant surfaces `UZ-GRANT-003` to the zombie on its next execute attempt against the affected service.

parameters.name: grant_id
parameters.description: Opaque grant ID returned by integration-requests.

post.description: Called by a zombie (or on its behalf) to request permission to use a third-party integration (e.g. Slack, GitHub). Creates a pending grant that a human approves via the grant-approval webhook.

get.description: Returns execution telemetry for a specific zombie — last activity, event count, budget consumed, error counts.

schema.description: Telemetry payload (shape evolves with operator needs).

post.summary: Post a chat message to a zombie
post.description: Build an EventEnvelope with `actor=steer:<user>` / `event_type=chat` and XADD it onto `zombie:{id}:events`. The Redis stream entry id IS the canonical event_id and is returned to the caller so SSE consumers can filter frames for the message they just sent. Same single-ingress contract as webhook and continuation events — no separate "queued vs instant" mode.

message.description: Operator message body. Up to 8192 bytes. The server wraps it in a JSON object with a "message" field as the envelope's request payload.

202.description: Event enqueued. The CLI uses event_id to filter SSE frames.

event_id.description: Redis stream entry id (the canonical event_id). Format `<ms>-<seq>`.

get.description: Cursor-paginated list of operator-visible events for one zombie, newest first. Each row is a `core.zombie_events` entry — the durable record produced by the worker write path on every steer, webhook, cron, or continuation event. Filter by actor glob (e.g. `steer:*`, `webhook:github`) or `since=` (Go-style duration like `2h`/`30m` or RFC 3339 timestamp). `since=` and `cursor=` are mutually exclusive.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque base64url-no-pad cursor returned in `next_cursor`. Mutually exclusive with `since`.

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description: Glob filter against the row's actor. `*` is a wildcard. Examples - 'steer:kishore', 'webhook:github', 'webhook:*'.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound on `created_at`. Accepts Go-style durations (15s/30m/2h/7d) or RFC 3339 timestamps. Mutually exclusive with `cursor`.

get.summary: Subscribe to a zombie's live activity stream (SSE)
get.description: Server-Sent Events tail of the Redis pub/sub channel `zombie:{id}:activity`. The handler SUBSCRIBEs on a dedicated Redis connection and forwards each PUBLISH frame as one SSE event with id (per-connection monotonic counter), event (frame kind), and data (JSON payload) lines. Frame kinds - event_received, tool_call_started, tool_call_progress, chunk, tool_call_completed, event_complete. Sequence ids reset to 0 on every new SUBSCRIBE and the server ignores `Last-Event-ID`. Clients reconnect by calling GET /events with a cursor to backfill the durable record, then re-open this stream. Authentication this slice is Bearer-only (CLI / programmatic). Browser cookie auth lands with the dashboard slice.

delete.summary: Kill the zombie's current running action
delete.description: Transitions the zombie's status from `active` or `paused` to `stopped` and records a `zombie_stopped` activity event. Modeled as a DELETE on a singleton sub-resource (the zombie's "current run") rather than the retired `POST .../stop` path-verb form — see `docs/REST_API_DESIGN_GUIDELINES.md` §7. Returns 409 if the zombie is already stopped or killed. Returns 404 if the zombie is not in the path workspace (cross-workspace IDOR guard).

get.summary: List a workspace's aggregate event history
get.description: Cursor-paginated list of operator-visible events across every zombie in the workspace, newest first. Replaces the deleted activity endpoint. Optional `zombie_id` filter narrows to one zombie. Same query shape as the per-zombie endpoint - `actor` glob, `since=` (duration or RFC 3339), and a `cursor` mutually exclusive with `since`.

parameters.name: zombie_id
parameters.description: Drill down to one zombie within the workspace.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a prior response. Mutually exclusive with `since`.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Lower bound on `created_at`. Go-style duration or RFC 3339 timestamp.

get.summary: List pending approval gates for a workspace
get.description:
Returns approval gates oldest-first (oldest is most urgent). Each row
surfaces the agent's proposed action, gathered evidence, blast-radius
assessment, and timeout countdown. Filter by zombie, gate kind, or
status. Cursor pagination over (requested_at, id) so concurrent
inserts don't cause silent skips.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Defaults to "pending". Supply "approved", "denied", "timed_out" to query terminal states.

get.summary: Get a single approval gate by id
get.description: Drives the dashboard detail page. 404 when the gate doesn't exist or belongs to a different workspace.

post.description:
Resolves the gate to "approved" and wakes the worker thread blocked
on the gate. Funnels through the channel-agnostic resolve core that
Slack callbacks and the auto-timeout sweeper share — first writer
wins; concurrent resolvers receive 409 with the original outcome and
resolver attribution.

Resolves the gate to "denied" and wakes the worker thread. The
worker treats `denied` as "do not execute the proposed tool call".
Same dedup semantics as `:approve`.

patch.description: Partial update of workspace fields. Today the only patchable surface is the pause/unpause toggle (`pause`, `reason`, `version`); future fields land on the same endpoint. Uses optimistic concurrency via the `version` field — a stale client gets 404.

pause.description: True to pause, false to unpause

reason.description: Human-readable reason for the state change

version.description: Optimistic concurrency version — must match current workspace version

200.description: Workspace updated (pause state changed when `pause` field present)

BearerAuth.description: Obtain a token via the CLI auth flow (POST /v1/auth/sessions) or GitHub OAuth

docs_uri.description: Stable link to documentation for this error code

title.description: Short human-readable label, same for every occurrence of this code

detail.description: Instance-specific context describing what went wrong
detail.example: No zombie with id 'abc123' in this workspace.

ReadyzBody.description: Response body for GET /readyz. Identical shape for both 200 (healthy) and 503 (degraded) responses — only the boolean values differ.

ready.description: Overall readiness — true only when every dependency is healthy. Mirrors the HTTP status (true ↔ 200, false ↔ 503).

database.description: True if the PostgreSQL connection pool is reachable and a trivial query succeeds

queue.description: True if the Redis queue backend is reachable

PlatformKey.description: A platform-wide LLM provider key configuration

source_workspace_id.description: Workspace whose BYOK key is used

active.description: Whether this platform key is active

TenantApiKey.description: Metadata for a tenant API key. `key_hash` and the raw key are never returned.

last_used_at.description: Stays null until the async-stamping workstream ships.

gate_kind.description: Classification driving UI grouping. Empty string when not classified.

proposed_action.description: Human-readable summary of what the zombie wants to do.

evidence.description: Agent-gathered context (free-form). Rendered as expandable JSON tree.

blast_radius.description: Agent's assessment of the action's reach.

detail.description: Optional reason text supplied by the resolver.

timeout_at.description: ms epoch — sweeper auto-denies after this point

updated_at.description: Set on terminal transition; doubles as resolved_at.

reason.description: Optional free-form note attached to the resolution.

ApprovalAlreadyResolvedResponse.description:
RFC 7807 problem+json with structured extension fields carrying the
original outcome and resolver attribution so the dashboard can render
"already resolved by X at Y" without a follow-up GET.

AgentProfile.description: Agent profile including trust and improvement metadata

trust_level.description: Current trust level (e.g. low, medium, high)

trust_streak_runs.description: Number of consecutive successful runs contributing to trust

improvement_stalled_warning.description: True if the agent's self-improvement has plateaued

AgentRelayRequest.description: Request body for Agent Relay LLM endpoints (spec template and preview). The workspace_id is provided via the path parameter.

tools.description: Optional tool definitions for the LLM to use during generation

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool's input
